name: Sweet Pastry
recipeInstructions:
1. If using butter in sticks, measure 7 tablespoons of butter.
2. The pastry is very sticky due to its high fat content. It's best made in a food processor.
3. Roll the chilled pieces of butter with flour to reduce stickiness.
4. Roll out on two sheets of Saran wrap, transfer into the flan pan, and peel off the wrap.
5. Add flour and sugar to the food processor bowl and pulse 1-2 times to aerate.
6. Roll cubes of butter in flour and distribute over flour and sugar in the work bowl.
7. Process briefly until the mixture reassembles into a fine meal.
8. Gently drop the egg through the chute while processing.
9. Continue processing briefly until the egg is fully incorporated.
10. Turn off the food processor, form the dough into a ball, and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.
11. Roll out on a sheet of Saran wrap-covered surface (add extra flour if needed).
12. Roll to a thickness of 1/8 inch and a diameter larger than the flan tin.
13. Carefully transfer the rolled pastry onto the flan pan, trim edges, and refrigerate for at least 1 hour before baking.
Preheat oven to 375°F. Place the lined tin in the center of the oven and bake for 15 minutes. If browning too much, reduce temperature to 325°F. Remove from the oven and allow it to cool before removing from the pan.
